item.title: BMG sues Anthropic for using Bruno Mars, Rolling Stones lyrics in AI training
item.description: Music company BMG Rights Management has sued artificial intelligence company Anthropic in ​California federal court for allegedly using its copyrighted lyrics ‌to train the large language models powering its Claude chatbot.

item.title: US exempts four foreign-made drones from import ban
item.description: The U.S. Federal Communications Commission said on Wednesday it was exempting imports of four ​foreign-made drones from a sweeping import ban adopted in December.

item.title: Trade Desk drops after report that Publicis advised clients against using its platform
item.description: Advertising technology firm Trade Desk's shares tumbled on Wednesday as Wall Street analysts downgraded the stock following a report that French ad giant Publicis ​Groupe advised its clients against using the company's media buying platform.

item.title: Crypto exchange Kraken freezes IPO plans, CoinDesk reports
item.description: Cryptocurrency exchange Kraken ​has put its multibillion-dollar ‌initial public offering plan on hold, CoinDesk reported on Wednesday, ​citing two people with ​knowledge of the matter

item.title: Google to allow AI opt-out to ease UK competition concerns
item.description: Google said on Wednesday it ​was developing new search controls to let websites opt out of ‌its generative AI features, as the U.S. tech giant sought to address the British competition regulator's concerns about its dominance in search services.

item.title: X down for thousands of users in the US, Downdetector shows
item.description: Social media ​platform X was ‌down for thousands of users in the U.S. ​on Wednesday, according ​to Downdetector.com.

item.title: Telegram still not complying with Russian law, Russian regulator says
item.description: Telegram messaging app is still ​not complying with ‌Russian laws, the state communications regulator Roskomnadzor said ​on Wednesday, ​according to state‑run news agency ⁠RIA.

item.title: US factory orders barely rise in January
item.description: New orders for U.S. factory goods edged up in January as weakness in transportation equipment partially offset gains elsewhere, government data showed on Wednesday.

item.title: Iran war energy shock sparks global push to reduce fossil fuel dependence
item.description: The energy shock from the Iran war has policymakers around the globe rethinking ways to reduce long-term dependence on oil and gas imports, with proposals to expand nuclear energy and renewables, grow strategic stockpiles and domestic production, and diversify foreign sources of supply.

item.title: EU lawmakers support ban on AI apps generating explicit images
item.description: Key EU lawmakers, who are influential in shaping changes to Europe's AI Act, on ​Wednesday backed a ban on AI apps which ‌create unauthorised sexually explicit images and urged that the bloc's landmark rules agreed two years ago include this ban.

item.title: Streaming boosts global music revenues once again in 2025, report shows
item.description: Boosted by paid streaming services, global recorded music revenues grew 6.4% to $31.7 billion ‌in 2025, their 11th straight year of growth, the IFPI organisation representing the recording industry worldwide said on Wednesday, while also urging action on streaming fraud.

item.title: US waives sanctions on deals involving Venezuela's PDVSA
item.description: The ​United States has issued a general ‌license authorizing certain deals involving Venezuela's state oil company PDVSA, the U.S. Treasury ​Department said on Wednesday.

item.title: Researchers uncover iPhone spyware capable of penetrating millions of devices
item.description: A powerful software exploit capable of ​penetrating and stealing information from potentially hundreds of millions of Apple iPhones was planted ‌on dozens of websites in Ukraine in recent weeks, researchers said on Wednesday.

item.title: GM says gas price hikes not impacting sales
item.description: General Motors hasn't seen much ​shift in sales following gas ‌price increases as a result of the war in Iran, the ​automaker's finance chief said on ​Wednesday.

item.title: UniCredit CEO says pan-European ambitions take precedence over Italian consolidation
item.description: A potential combination with Commerzbank is currently the priority for UniCredit though further consolidation in Italy could still provide opportunities in ​the future, CEO Andrea Orcel said on Wednesday.

item.title: Belgium's imec secures rare ASML High NA EUV tool to drive next-generation chips
item.description: Belgian chip research lab imec said ‌on Wednesday it has secured an ASML $400-million High NA EUV lithography machine--one of fewer than a dozen worldwide--cementing its role in preparing next-generation chipmaking tools for industry ​use.

item.title: US producer prices surge in February on services
item.description: U.S. producer prices increased more than expected and could accelerate further as the war in the Middle East boosts oil prices ​and the import pass-through persists.

item.title: Electronics firm Jabil raises annual forecasts on data center demand
item.description: Electronic component maker Jabil raised its full-year profit and revenue forecasts on Wednesday, betting on ​strong demand for infrastructure services from AI data centers.

item.title: BMW eyes stabilisation in China with latest 'Neue Klasse' launch
item.description: Prices are stabilising in China after a period of heavy discounting in the electric ​vehicle market, BMW sales chief Jochen Goller said ‌on Wednesday.

item.title: ECB sees underpriced geopolitical risks, warns against easing bank rules
item.description: Financial markets are underpricing geopolitical risks, increasing the potential for sudden sell-offs, European Central Bank supervisor ​Claudia Buch said on Wednesday, as she warned ‌against easing bank regulations.

item.title: Fed's Treasury bill buying on track to moderate, amid work to rejigger bond holdings
item.description: The Federal Reserve’s work to manage challenges around its balance sheet is proving to be a rare island of calm as war and broader economic issues complicate the monetary policy outlook.

item.title: UK to examine labelling AI content among wider copyright reforms
item.description: Britain plans to consider requiring labels on AI-generated content to protect consumers from disinformation and deepfakes, the government said on ​Wednesday, as it outlined other areas of focus to tackle ‌the evolving global challenge.

item.title: Poland plans to ban mobile phone use by under-16s in schools
item.description: Poland plans to ban the use of mobile phones by ​under-16s in schools from September 1, ‌the education minister said on Wednesday, joining a growing list of countries moving to limit ​children's screen time and social ​media use.

item.title: EU Inc proposal seeks to rival US in innovation by easing startup creation
item.description: The European Commission proposed on ​Wednesday allowing firms to set up in as little as 48 hours and operate according to a ‌single set of rules across the 27-nation EU in a bid to narrow the gap with the United States in innovative startups.

item.title: General Mills reaffirms full-year sales, profit forecasts
item.description: General Mills reaffirmed its annual sales and profit forecast ​on Wednesday after slashing it ‌last month, as pressure on consumer spending and stiff competition muted demand for the ​Cheerios maker's pantry staples and ​snacks.

item.title: US to sell oil and gas leases in Alaska petroleum reserve for first time since 2019
item.description: The U.S. government on Wednesday will hold a sale of oil and gas drilling rights in Alaska's National Petroleum ​Reserve for the first time since 2019, the latest test ‌of the industry's appetite for acreage in the state.

item.title: Macy's forecasts weak annual profit; sees tariff relief
item.description: Macy's on Wednesday forecast weaker‑than‑expected annual profit as shoppers rein in spending, though the U.S. department store chain said the ​hit from import tariffs should ease in the second half ‌of the year.

item.title: Morning Bid: Fed plot unfolds
item.description: What matters in U.S. and global markets today

item.title: UK watchdog tightens cyber incident reporting rules as attacks surge
item.description: Britain's finance regulator confirmed ​new incident and ‌third-party reporting rules on Wednesday, giving ​firms 12 ​months to prepare for ⁠clearer requirements ​aimed at strengthening ​resilience against cyber attacks and third-party disruptions.

item.title: Greek firms scan computer systems as Iran war raises cyberattack risks, sources say
item.description: Greek shipowners and other companies are scanning their computer systems ‌for evidence of cyberattacks after advice from the National Cybersecurity Authority, two sources said on Wednesday following incidents that have been linked to the Iran war.
